name: Amazon EC2
flavor: Virtual machines on demand. Pay only for what you run, scale up/down quickly, and deploy globally. Entry point for AWS customer acquisition and long-term infrastructure spend.

name: Amazon S3
flavor: Object storage for files and data at any scale. Used for backups, app assets, and data lakes because it's durable, low cost, and integrates across AWS.

name: AWS Lambda
flavor: Run code in response to events without managing servers. Auto-scales and charges per request/time, lowering ops work and cost for bursty workloads and integrations.

name: Amazon RDS
flavor: Managed relational databases (MySQL/Postgres/etc.). Automates provisioning, backups, patching, and scaling so teams cut DBA effort, improve reliability, and ship faster.

name: Amazon DynamoDB
flavor: Serverless NoSQL database built for low-latency at scale. Avoids capacity planning while keeping predictable performance as traffic grows.

name: AWS IAM
flavor: Identity and access management for AWS. Defines who/what can do which actions; least-privilege reduces breach risk and meets compliance needs.

name: Amazon VPC
flavor: Isolated virtual network in AWS. Control subnets, routing, and connectivity (VPN/Direct Connect) to meet security, latency, and compliance requirements.

name: Amazon CloudFront
flavor: CDN for websites, video, and APIs. Caches near users to cut latency, handle spikes, and reduce origin load and cost.

name: Amazon Route 53
flavor: DNS and traffic routing with health checks. Routes users to healthy endpoints and supports failover to improve uptime and customer experience.

name: Amazon CloudWatch
flavor: Metrics, logs, and alarms for AWS and apps. Detect issues early, shorten incidents, and track service health and business KPIs.

name: Amazon EKS
flavor: Managed Kubernetes control plane. Standardize container platforms with less ops burden while using Kubernetes tooling and ecosystem.

name: Amazon ECS
flavor: AWS-native container orchestration. Run microservices with simpler setup and strong AWS integration, reducing operational overhead.

name: AWS Fargate
flavor: Serverless compute for containers. Run tasks without managing servers, pay for resources used, and scale easily - good for teams that want containers with minimal ops.

name: Amazon API Gateway
flavor: Create, secure, and operate APIs. Handles auth, throttling, caching, and monitoring to launch APIs safely and control cost.

name: Amazon SQS
flavor: Managed message queue to decouple services. Buffers bursts, smooths processing, and prevents cascading failures.

name: Amazon SNS
flavor: Publish/subscribe messaging for fan-out notifications. Route events to many targets to build responsive, loosely coupled systems.

name: AWS KMS
flavor: Managed encryption keys and policies. Central control and audit trails help protect data and meet regulatory requirements.

name: AWS CloudTrail
flavor: Audit log of AWS actions. Shows who changed what and when for compliance, investigations, and governance.

name: Amazon Aurora
flavor: Cloud-native relational database for MySQL/Postgres. High performance and availability with less admin work for critical apps at scale.

name: Amazon EBS
flavor: Block storage for EC2. Provides predictable performance for databases and apps; easy to resize and snapshot for reliability.

name: Amazon EventBridge
flavor: Event bus for routing events across AWS and SaaS. Simplifies event-driven architectures and reduces brittle point-to-point links.

name: AWS Step Functions
flavor: Workflow orchestration with retries and state tracking. Reduce glue code and improve reliability for multi-step business processes.

name: AWS Organizations
flavor: Manage many AWS accounts with centralized policies and consolidated billing. Core for governance, cost allocation, and security at scale.

name: AWS Secrets Manager
flavor: Store and rotate secrets (passwords/tokens). Reduces credential leaks and manual rotation work, strengthening security posture.

name: AWS WAF
flavor: Web application firewall. Blocks common exploits and bots with managed rules to reduce fraud and downtime.

name: AWS Systems Manager
flavor: Operations automation for fleets: patching, run commands, inventory, and configuration. Standardize ops and reduce toil across environments.

name: AWS Inferentia
flavor: AWS chips optimized for AI inference. Lower cost and latency for serving models in production at scale.

name: AWS Graviton
flavor: AWS-designed CPUs with strong price-performance. Often lowers compute cost for common workloads at scale.

name: AWS Control Tower
flavor: Automates a governed multi-account landing zone. Sets guardrails and best practices so organizations can scale AWS safely.

name: Amazon GuardDuty
flavor: Threat detection that analyzes AWS signals and logs. Flags suspicious behavior quickly to reduce impact of compromises.

name: AWS Lake Formation
flavor: Build and govern data lakes with centralized permissions and catalogs. Speeds analytics while reducing data access risk.

name: AWS Security Hub
flavor: Central view of security findings across AWS. Prioritize remediation and standardize controls at scale.

name: AWS Shield
flavor: DDoS protection for internet-facing apps. Helps maintain availability during attacks and reduces response burden.

name: AWS CloudFormation
flavor: Infrastructure as code using templates. Create consistent environments, reduce misconfigurations, and enable repeatable deployments.

name: Amazon EC2
flavor: Virtual machines on demand. Pay only for what you run, scale up/down quickly, and deploy globally. Entry point for AWS customer acquisition and long-term infrastructure spend.

name: Amazon S3
flavor: Object storage for files and data at any scale. Used for backups, app assets, and data lakes because it's durable, low cost, and integrates across AWS.

name: AWS Lambda
flavor: Run code in response to events without managing servers. Auto-scales and charges per request/time, lowering ops work and cost for bursty workloads and integrations.

name: Amazon Bedrock
flavor: Build generative AI apps with foundation models via API. Adds governance and security controls so businesses can ship AI features faster while managing risk.

name: Amazon SageMaker
flavor: End-to-end platform to build, train, and deploy ML models. MLOps tools reduce time from prototype to production and improve consistency and compliance.

name: Amazon Q Developer
flavor: AI assistant for developers. Helps write code, explain errors, and generate docs to improve productivity and shorten cycle time.

name: AWS Trainium
flavor: AWS chips optimized for training large AI models. Reduce training cost and increase throughput for large-scale experimentation.

name: Amaozn Quick Suite
flavor: An AI workspace for nontechnical teams that can draft docs and emails, summarize meetings, answer questions from your files, and automate simple requests—all from plain-language prompts.

name: Amazon Connect
flavor: A cloud contact center for customer support. Teams can set up phone and chat help, route customers to the right agent, see customer details, and track service quality—all without running call-center hardware.

name: Customer Obsession
flavor: Leaders start with the customer and work backwards. They earn and keep customer trust.

name: Ownership
flavor: Leaders act on behalf of the entire company. They never say 'that's not my job.'

name: Invent and Simplify
flavor: Leaders expect and require innovation and invention. They simplify to move faster.

name: Are Right, A Lot
flavor: Leaders have strong judgment and good instincts. They seek diverse perspectives and work to disconfirm beliefs.

name: Learn and Be Curious
flavor: Leaders are never done learning. They improve themselves and explore new possibilities.

name: Hire and Develop the Best
flavor: Leaders raise the performance bar with every hire and develop others to be leaders.

name: Think Big
flavor: Leaders create and communicate a bold direction that inspires results. They think differently.

name: Insist on the Highest Standards
flavor: Leaders have relentlessly high standards and drive quality that customers notice.

name: Earn Trust
flavor: Leaders listen attentively, speak candidly, and treat others respectfully.

name: Dive Deep
flavor: Leaders operate at all levels, stay connected to details, and audit frequently.

name: Have Backbone; Disagree and Commit
flavor: Leaders challenge decisions when they disagree, then commit fully once a decision is made.

name: Deliver Results
flavor: Leaders focus on key inputs and deliver results with the right quality and in a timely fashion.

name: Strive to be Earth's Best Employer
flavor: Leaders work every day to create a safer, more productive, and more inclusive workplace.

name: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
flavor: Leaders consider the impact of their work and act responsibly as they grow.

name: Bias for Action
flavor: Speed matters. Many decisions are reversible and should be made quickly with reasonable judgment.

name: Frugality
flavor: Leaders accomplish more with less. Constraints drive resourcefulness and invention.

name: AWS re:Inforce
flavor: Security-focused AWS conference. Practical guidance on identity, governance, and incident readiness.

name: AWS Global Summits
flavor: Local events worldwide with keynotes and sessions. Meet the community and learn what's new.

name: AWS Innovate Online Conference
flavor: Online event series with focused tracks. Efficient way for teams to skill up quickly.

name: AWSome Day
flavor: Beginner-friendly AWS learning event. Great for onboarding teams into cloud fundamentals.

name: AWS re:Invent
flavor: Flagship AWS conference. Big launches, deep sessions, and the fastest way to learn what's changing.

name: Serverless Architecture
flavor: Design where the cloud handles scaling and infrastructure. Teams ship faster by focusing on code and events.

name: Block Storage
flavor: Disk-like storage for servers and databases. Prioritizes low latency and predictable performance.

name: Data Lake
flavor: Central store for raw and curated data. Enables analytics and AI by making data reusable across teams.

name: Zero Trust
flavor: Security model that verifies every request. Reduces blast radius by assuming no network is automatically trusted.

name: Regions & Availability Zones
flavor: AWS is split into Regions and independent Availability Zones. Choose Regions for latency/residency; design across AZs for high availability and fault isolation.

name: Shared Responsibility Model
flavor: AWS secures the cloud infrastructure; you secure identities, data, and configuration. Clear ownership prevents security gaps and makes audits faster and cleaner.

name: Multi-Account Strategy
flavor: Use multiple AWS accounts to isolate workloads, separate billing, and apply guardrails. Limits blast radius and simplifies governance as organizations scale.

name: Infrastructure as Code
flavor: Define infrastructure in code so environments are repeatable, reviewable, and automated. Reduces human error and enables safe, fast change management.

name: GraphQL
flavor: API approach where clients request exactly the fields they need. Can reduce payloads and speed product iteration.

name: OLAP Analytics
flavor: Analytics optimized for fast reporting across large datasets. Supports dashboards and business decision-making.

name: Defense in Depth
flavor: Layer security controls across identity, network, encryption, and monitoring. If one control fails, others still protect data and availability.

name: Elasticity
flavor: Scale resources up and down with demand. Avoid overbuying, handle spikes, and align cost with usage - core to AWS's pay-for-what-you-use model.

name: Type 1 / Type 2 Decisions
flavor: Type 1 decisions are hard to reverse - slow down. Type 2 are reversible - move fast and learn. Helps teams balance speed and risk.

name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
flavor: Combine a model with your documents at query time. Improves accuracy by grounding answers in trusted data.

name: Vector Databases
flavor: Store embeddings to search by meaning. Useful for semantic search, recommendations, and RAG pipelines.

name: Well-Architected Review
flavor: Structured review to find risks and improve designs across reliability, security, cost, and operations.

name: Coffee Station
flavor: One free coffee per day - choose wisely. A tiny perk that still fuels big launches, long meetings, and 'quick' syncs.

name: Yellow Badge
flavor: Contractor badge. Signals you're building with AWS teams, even if you're not on a blue badge yet - still expected to deliver and learn fast.

name: Blue Badge
flavor: Employee badge and a symbol of belonging. Also a reminder: you now own the pager, the docs, and the follow-through.

name: Day 1
flavor: Mindset of acting like it's the beginning: move fast, stay curious, and avoid complacency.

name: Six-Pager
flavor: Narrative doc used for decisions. Forces clear thinking, tradeoffs, and alignment before teams build.

name: Doppler Building
flavor: A well-known Seattle campus building. Office lore meets cloud scale - people still love a recognizable landmark.

name: PRFAQ
flavor: Write the press release and FAQ first. Clarifies the customer value before building anything.

name: Working Backwards
flavor: Start from the customer problem, then design the solution. Keeps teams aligned on real value, not features.

name: Bar Raiser
flavor: Hiring role focused on keeping the talent bar high. Reinforces long-term quality over short-term speed.

name: On-Call Pager
flavor: The moment you learn what 'ownership' feels like. Reliability work that keeps customers happy and teams honest.

name: Chime Call
flavor: Meetings happen. A lot. The unofficial soundtrack of collaboration, reviews, and quick syncs.

name: Banana Cart
flavor: A legendary perk... as long as you like bananas. A loving nod to frugality: keep costs tight, invest where customers feel it.

name: The Spheres
flavor: Iconic Seattle workspace built around plants. A fun symbol that builder energy and environment matter - and a photo everyone recognizes.

name: HQ2 Helix
flavor: A bold, iconic building concept. A playful symbol of scale, ambition, and the occasional obsession with big bets.
